items.title: Hero MotoCorp shares in focus as Q3 profit jumps 14%, board declares Rs 110 interim dividend
items.description: Hero MotoCorp reported a 14% year-on-year rise in Q3FY26 consolidated profit to Rs 1,268 crore, driven by strong volumes and better realisations, with revenue jumping 22% to Rs 12,487 crore. The company also announced an interim dividend of Rs 110 per share and a Rs 275 crore investment in Euler Motors.

items.title: ETMarkets Smart Talk: Amit Gupta on why STT hike may cool speculative F&O trades without hurting investors
items.description: A recent hike in Securities Transaction Tax on derivatives aims to curb excessive speculation. Experts believe this move will impact high-frequency, low-margin trades. Long-term investors are unlikely to be affected. Valuations, earnings, and currency stability are key factors for foreign investor flows. The market faces a challenging period requiring balanced preparation.

items.title: Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: Emami, Solar Industries among 5 stocks that could give 10-50% return
items.description: Brokerage recommendations across defence manufacturing, housing finance, FMCG, and staffing services point to selective but compelling opportunities, with analysts highlighting strong earnings visibility and valuation comfort in several names.We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources

items.title: Sensex falls over 200 pts, Nifty below 25,600 ahead of RBI MPC decision; mid, smallcaps slip
items.description: Indian stock markets, Nifty and Sensex, began Friday trading with slight declines. This marks a second consecutive session of losses for the benchmark indices. Investors are adopting a cautious stance. This is due to the upcoming Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee policy announcement later today. Global market sentiment also appears subdued.

items.title: RVNL shares in focus after Q3 profit rises 4% YoY to Rs 324 crore
items.description: Shares of Rail Vikas Nigam Ltd (RVNL) will be in focus on February 6 after the state-owned rail infrastructure firm reported a modest 4% year-on-year rise in Q3FY26 net profit to Rs 324 crore. Revenue grew 3% to Rs 4,684 crore, while higher expenses capped margin expansion despite an increase in total income.

items.title: British International invests Rs 90 crore in UC Impower Fund
items.description: British International Investment has invested Rs 90 crore in UC Impower, an India-focused early growth-stage equity fund, marking the largest commitment to the Rs 500 crore vehicle. The fund backs financial services and climate solution firms. UC Impower has raised about Rs 435 crore so far and plans to build a portfolio of 10–11 investments.

items.title: Bharti Airtel shares in focus after Q3 profit drops 55% YoY to Rs 6,630 crore. Buy, sell or hold?
items.description: Bharti Airtel shares are in focus after a sharp year-on-year decline in profit despite strong revenue, EBITDA, and ARPU growth. India operations, home business expansion, lower capex, and reduced net debt supported performance. Brokerages remain positive, citing robust cash flows, subscriber additions, and improving operational metrics.

items.title: Exclusive | Why BofA's Amish Shah is booking profit in defence stocks after Budget
items.description: BofA Global Research’s Amish Shah is booking profits in defence stocks after a sharp rally, citing stretched valuations despite a strong Budget capex push. Shah prefers rotating into rate-sensitive cyclicals and sees Nifty at 29,000 by December 2026, with earnings growth expected to accelerate in FY27.

items.title: Suzlon Energy shares in focus after Q3 profit rises 15% YoY to Rs 445 crore
items.description: Suzlon Energy reported a 15% year-on-year rise in Q3FY26 profit to Rs 445 crore, supported by strong revenue growth, higher volumes, and margin expansion. The company posted record deliveries, a robust order book, and a net cash position, reinforcing confidence in its clean energy growth strategy.

items.title: LIC shares climb 4% after Q3 results. Should you buy, sell, or hold?
items.description: LIC shares surged 4% following a 17% year-on-year profit jump to Rs 12,930 crore in Q3FY26. The insurer also reported a 17% rise in net premium income to Rs 1.26 lakh crore. Bernstein maintained a Market-Perform rating with a Rs 940 target price.

items.title: Sebi proposes tighter rules for single-stock derivatives strategy
items.description: India's market regulator Sebi is proposing new margin rules for single-stock derivatives. The changes will affect trading strategies involving different expiry dates. Specifically, benefits from offsetting positions will not be available on the day a contract expires. This move aims to reduce risks for traders and trading members.

items.title: Tata Motors PV shares in focus after Q3 loss widens to Rs 3,486 crore on JLR cyber incident
items.description: Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles Ltd shares will be in spotlight reported a significant consolidated loss of Rs 3,486 crore in Q3, primarily due to a cyber incident at Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) which impacted revenue by 26%. Despite challenges, the company anticipates a strong recovery in the fourth quarter with JLR's normalization and continued domestic growth.

items.title: Technical snag at NSDL delays settlement of trades since Tuesday
items.description: A technical issue at National Securities Depository has disrupted share settlements for three days. Investors' purchased shares are not appearing in their demat accounts. This prevents them from selling these holdings. The problem stems from NSDL's inability to process inter-depository transfers. This impacts the routine movement of securities between depositories. Operations are delayed as a result.

items.title: Select stocks can gain 10-37% on Budget, trade deal boost
items.description: Top 15 high conviction stock ideas from leading brokerages following the key events

items.title: Gift Nifty falls over 100 pts; here's trading setup for the day
items.description: Indian stock markets moved sideways on Thursday. Investors adopted a cautious stance, awaiting new domestic cues. The Nifty index remained within a narrow band, struggling to break out. Analysts anticipate continued range-bound trading for the index. Select individual stocks may show strength. The India VIX, a fear gauge, saw a slight decrease.

items.title: Stocks in news: Tata Steel, Hero MotoCorp, Bharti Airtel, RIL, Nykaa
items.description: Markets experienced a dip on Thursday, with analysts suggesting a buy-on-dips strategy for the Nifty above 25,400. Several companies like Tata Steel, Hero MotoCorp, Nykaa, Bharti Airtel, Tata Motors PV, RIL, and RVNL are in focus due to their recent financial results and business developments.

items.title: Fractal Analytics IPO opens Feb 9; GMP signals 7% listing gain. Key details here
items.description: Fractal Analytics, a leading Indian AI firm, is launching a Rs 2,833.90 crore IPO on February 9, marking India's first pure-play AI company to go public. With a grey market premium suggesting a potential 7% listing gain, the issue aims to fund growth and innovation. Investors can participate in India's burgeoning AI sector.

items.title: Meesho slides 40% from peak, slips below listing price. Here is why brokerages still see 26% upside
items.description: Meesho shares have fallen nearly 40% from their peak after Q3 losses widened sharply, slipping below the listing price. Despite near-term margin pressures and concerns over growth sustainability, foreign brokerages UBS and BofA remain constructive, citing strong revenue growth, improving logistics efficiency and long-term user expansion.

items.title: Hitachi Energy India shares soar 14% as Q3 net profit jumps 90%. Should you buy, sell or hold?
items.description: Hitachi Energy India shares surged 14% after the company reported a 90% year-on-year jump in Q3 net profit, aided by strong execution, margin expansion and a record order backlog. Brokerages remain bullish, citing earnings visibility, robust order flows and improving operating leverage.

items.title: Amazon shares crash over 11% after-market hours. What triggered the sell-off in FAANG stock
items.description: Amazon’s stock fell more than 11% in after-hours trading after the company said it would raise capital spending by nearly 60% to about $200 billion this year, sparking investor concerns over rising AI-related costs. The decline came despite strong fourth-quarter results, with revenue rising 14% to $213.4 billion.

items.title: Indian IT stocks set for worst week in four months as AI jitters deepen
items.description: In a dramatic turn of events, Indian software shares fell sharply this Friday, leading to their toughest week in over four months. The spotlight is on artificial intelligence anxieties, which are weighing heavy on the high-margin income of IT companies.

items.title: Stocks with a 3- to 5-year perspective. 7 stocks where the budget continues to provide tailwinds by way of policy support
items.description: Defence, railways, power. The common thread running through these sectors is that revival came only after the government brought about policy changes that prioritised them. However, unlike, for instance, power, where government support resulted in a relatively quick turnaround, there are some sectors where such support would be needed for a longer time. The fact that the government has, for the  third year in a row, made an effort in the budget clearly shows its intent.

items.title: Indian stocks enjoy a rare combination, makes a case for re-rating: Morgan Stanley's Ridham Desai
items.description: Morgan Stanley sees Indian equities entering a rare phase that supports a valuation re-rating, driven by policy stimulus, improving earnings, weak foreign positioning and attractive relative valuations. With macro stability improving and growth accelerating, the firm forecasts meaningful upside for the Sensex through 2026 under its base and bull case scenarios.

items.title: RBI promises pre-emptive liquidity action to ensure policy transmission; keeps repo rate at 5.25%
items.description: The Reserve Bank of India maintained the repo rate at 5.25% and will proactively manage liquidity to ensure effective monetary policy transmission. The central bank also plans to remove the Rs 2.5 lakh crore limit for investments under the Voluntary Retention Route, while projecting comfortable inflation for FY26.

items.title: Why Tata Motors’ India PV business may not offset JLR’s cyber drag: Jefferies explains
items.description: Jefferies highlights significant pressures on Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) following Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles' weak December quarter, with JLR's EBITDA plunging 91% YoY. Despite improved India PV prospects, JLR faces structural challenges, including competition and EV transition, impacting overall profitability and growth.

items.title: MRF declares Rs 3 interim dividend, February 13 set as record date
items.description: Tyre maker MRF announced a second interim dividend of Rs 3 per share for the financial year ending March 31, 2026. This follows a robust Q3 earnings report. Net profit surged significantly, driven by strong operational performance and improved business execution. Revenue saw a healthy increase, with EBITDA jumping considerably. Margins also expanded sharply, reflecting efficient operations.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei rallies on earnings in unsteady trade before Sunday election
items.description: The impact of earnings, recent sharp share declines, and the weekend's impending election contributed ‍to a ⁠sense of tug-of-war in ⁠Tokyo's equity markets, said Maki Sawada, an equities strategist at Nomura Securities.

items.title: UPI stocks Paytm, Mobikwik fall up to 4% after RBI proposes transaction fraud framework
items.description: UPI stocks Paytm and Mobikwik fell up to 4% after the RBI proposed a framework to compensate customers for small value fraudulent transactions. The central bank plans to cap compensation at Rs 25,000 while tightening customer liability norms and enhancing digital payments safety through stricter safeguards.

items.title: ITC, Godfrey Phillips, other cigarette stocks jump up to 13% amid reports of price hike
items.description: Cigarette company shares saw a significant jump on Friday. This surge followed reports of price increases, with firms like ITC, Godfrey Phillips, and VST Industries passing on higher costs to consumers. The market also saw value buying after a recent tax overhaul. Higher priced cigarette packets have been introduced, with price hikes ranging from 15% to 30%.

items.title: BEML Q3: Loss widens to Rs 22 crore YoY despite 24% revenue growth; Rs 2.5 dividend
items.description: Total expenses during the quarter rose sharply to Rs 1,113 crore, up from Rs 796 crore in Q2FY26 and Rs 851 crore in Q3FY25, reflecting a 40% sequential increase and a 31% year-on-year rise. The higher costs were driven by employee benefits, raw material expenses, and finance costs, among other factors.

items.title: MRF shares surge 9% after Q3 profit doubles, dividend announcement
items.description: MRF shares jumped up to 9% after strong December quarter results, driven by doubled profits, margin expansion, and revenue growth. Improved operating leverage and a second interim dividend boosted investor confidence despite higher costs in a volatile cost environment market.

items.title: Market Wrap: Sensex adds 266 pts, Nifty above 25,650; Indian rupee logs best week in over 3 years
items.description: Indian stock markets, Nifty and Sensex, ended Friday higher. The Reserve Bank of India maintained its policy repo rate at 5.25 percent. Cigarette stocks saw a significant surge following price increases. Global markets retreated, impacted by a Wall Street selloff and AI concerns. Crude oil futures extended losses.

items.title: 14 consistent high-margin stocks rally up to 430% in a year. Do you own any?
items.description: In a market often driven by noise and short-term excitement, a quiet group of companies has been steadily delivering results. Around 170 stocks have managed a rare feat—posting profit margins above 10% for four consecutive quarters, up to the quarter ended December '25. To focus on businesses with scale and staying power, we narrowed the BSE universe to companies with a market capitalisation exceeding Rs 2,500 crore, while excluding banking and financial names.Nearly 40 stocks from this refined list have rewarded shareholders with over 20% gains in the past year. Digging deeper, we spotlighted 14 standout names that have delivered spectacular rallies ranging from 50% to an eye-catching 430%—including four stocks that truly created wealth for investors within a one-year timeframe. Companies posting double-digit profit margins consistently over the last four quarters indicate operational efficiency, stable demand, and strong profitability discipline. (Data Source: ACE Equity).

items.title: Anand Rathi reports Rs 13 crore fraud by unknown individuals, group company employees
items.description: Anand Rathi Share and Stock Brokers on Friday informed the exchanges that its Internal Inquiry Committee has discovered a Rs 13 crore fraud. The brokerage said that the crime has been committed by certain unknown individuals in connivance with employees of group company Anand Rathi IT Private Limited.

items.title: PFC announces merger with REC in line with Budget 2026 roadmap
items.description: Power Finance Corporation's board has approved a merger with Rural Electrification Corporation. This move aligns with the Union Budget 2026 roadmap. The restructuring aims to achieve scale and improve efficiency in public sector power financiers. The merger will consolidate both entities into a single balance sheet. This significant step is set to reshape India's power financing landscape.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: LIC, Godfrey Phillips among 6 stocks that hogged limelight on Friday
items.description: Indian headline indices ended in the green amid buying action in consumer and private banks though the gains were capped because of selling pressure in auto, IT and pharma stocks. While Nifty ended at 25,693.70, rising 50.90 points or 0.20%, the BSE Sensex marched ahead by 266.47 points or 0.32% to 83,580.40.Here are 6 stocks that saw action today
